checklist: Define Big O, Big Omega, and Big Theta
Compare log N, linear N, quadratic N^2, and exponential 2^N

states.question: If an algorithm runs in 3N^2 + 10N + 5 operations, what is its Big O complexity?
states.explanation: Asymptotic notation focuses on the dominant term (highest power of N) as N approaches infinity, ignoring constants.

states.analogy_description: Cleaning N rooms in a hotel scales linearly O(N). Sweeping a single lobby takes O(1) time. Scrubbing every room against every other room to check for dust pairs scales quadratically O(N^2).

whiteboard_data.title: Asymptotic Growth Curve
whiteboard_data.description: As input size N scales to infinity, quadratic growth N^2 dominates linear growth N.

steps.details: Quadratic growth rate dwarfs linear as N scales.

states.exercise_description: Write a function `get_first_element(lst)` that returns the first element of a list. If the list is empty, return `None` (Demonstrates O(1) time).
Write a function `contains_value(lst, target)` that checks if a list contains a target value by scanning it (Demonstrates O(N) time).

states.prompt: Explain Asymptotic Notation in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.back: Worst-case upper bound of runtime growth rate.
Best-case lower bound of runtime growth rate.
